commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh For as long as the son of Jesse lives upon the ground, you shall not be established, nor your kingdom. Therefore now send and fetch him unto me, for he shall surely die. The son of death. That is, one that deserveth death, and shall surely be put to death. (Challoner) So people are often styled sons of perdition, of hell, of light, (Calmet) when they are worthy of such things. (Haydock) All the crime of David, was his too exalted merit, which, under a jealous prince, is often fatal. Nec minus periculum ex magnâ famâ, quam ex malâ. (Tacitus, Agricola.

commentaries.author: Richard Challoner
commentaries.content: The son of death: That is, one that deserveth death, and shall surely be put to death.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
